issue_title: Meta issue: "Tag not found" error when using `deis pull`
issue_body:
This issue is meant to coordinate the discussion of the "Tag not found" error some users see when using `deis pull`. This issue seemed to have two different root causes which manifested in the same way, making it difficult to resolve:

* The deis-registry component sometimes timed out when pulling the layers from the remote registry. This was resolved in https://github.com/deis/docker-registry/pull/10 which shipped in Deis v1.4.0.
* The deis-cache component, which was configured to be used as an LRU cache for registry in #1858, seems to be configured to only use [50MB](https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/8826c83b28a573334083edc83571488238857924/cache/main.go#L23) of memory, which is suspected to be too little to effectively serve as an LRU cache.

Users who are still seeing this issue after upgrading to Deis v1.4.0 are encourgaed to try disabling registry's LRU cache. This is achieved by logging into the host which runs `deis-registry` and:
```console
$ nse deis-registry
$ vi /app/templates/config.yml
$ # comment the lines for cache_lru
$ /app/bin/reload
```

If `deis pull` works reliably after this for some time, it points to the cache as the culprit. We have not been able to confirm this in our testing, so we can't resolve this until we have some confidence in the fix. If cache is indeed the culprit, we should consider bumping its memory (although we'd be choosing an arbitrary value that may also be too small), or removing the registry's LRU cache entirely. We should ideally do some testing to see what effect (if any) the registry LRU cache has on performance.

/cc @johanneswuerbach 

refs #3116
refs #2916

issue_title: Disk cleaning does not remove all old images...
issue_body:
So after running the recovery command described in http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/managing_deis/disk_usage/ I still did not reclaim enough disk space to my taste.

I ran docker images to see what was still left.

~~~
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ docker images                        
REPOSITORY                            TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             VIRTUAL SIZE
10.133.120.13:5000/bplpp-demos        v11                 39adeac10e77        4 hours ago         1.553 GB
10.133.120.13:5000/bplpp-demos        v9                  67e0e15af1a9        5 hours ago         1.553 GB
10.133.120.13:5000/bplpp-demos        v8                  f16bb09913ff        5 hours ago         1.553 GB
~~~

Is an extract
So clearly we should be able to remove at least v8 and v9 from this list. Lets try to do so.

~~~
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ docker rmi f16bb09913ff
Error response from daemon: Conflict, cannot delete f16bb09913ff because the container c7e2b1883020 is using it, use -f to force
Error: failed to remove images: [f16bb09913ff]
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ 
~~~

Lets see if we can find this container...

~~~
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ docker ps | grep c7e2b1883020
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ 
~~~

nope we couldn't. Lets try something else

~~~
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ docker ps -a | grep c7e2b1883020
c7e2b1883020        10.133.120.13:5000/bplpp-demos:v8        "/runner/init start    5 hours ago         Exited (1) 5 hours ago                                                                                                 bplpp-demos_v8.clock.1         
core@ams3-cloud-beeple-3 ~ $ 
~~~~


Uhm, its exited, why would it not remove???

issue_title: [meta] Audit components for port usage.
issue_body:
All components deployed as part of the Deis platform should use standard ports wherever possible. Audit all cluster services and make sure default ports are used by default.

Refs deis/deis#4809

issue_title: Getting "list COMPONENTS should be sorted" even though they already appear to be
issue_body:
Running `catkin_lint -W2 /path/to/my/pkgs` results in:
```
my_pkg: CMakeLists.txt(4): notice: list COMPONENTS should be sorted
```

for the following snippet:
```cmake
find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS
  moveit_core
  pluginlib
  roscpp
  tf_conversions
)
```

As far as I can tell the `COMPONENTS` arguments are actually sorted alphabetically (and SublimeText seems to agree).

issue_title: API version should be set by the library instead of supplied by user
issue_body:
The comments and repo summary say to use v4 of the API, but, as reported in #43, that now fails with the current version of this library.

The library should have the supported API version in the code, instead of relying on it being provided by the user, so that a given release will always reference the API version that it works with.

issue_title: autoclass appears to construct classes multiple times during intialization?
issue_body:
I'm flailing about trying to use pyjnius to call functions from the [JDownloader project](http://jdownloader.org/knowledge/wiki/development/get-started).

Basically, I have a bunch of precompiled *.jar/*.class files, and I want to use functionality out of them. 

I'm executing the following script:

```

import logging
import os.path

log = logging.getLogger("Main.Jnius-Interface.Config")

jd_root = '/media/Storage/Scripts/jd'


class_path_entries = []

class_path_entries.append(os.path.join(jd_root, 'jd', 'plugins', 'decrypter'))
class_path_entries.append(os.path.join(jd_root, 'jd', 'plugins', 'hoster'))
class_path_entries.append(os.path.join(jd_root, 'libs'))
class_path_entries.append(os.path.join(jd_root, 'Core.jar'))
class_path_entries.append(os.path.join(jd_root, 'JDownloader.jar'))
class_path_entries.append(os.path.join(jd_root))

log.debug("Have %s classpath entries", len(class_path_entries))

import jnius_config
jnius_config.add_options('-Xrs', '-Xmx4096m')
jnius_config.set_classpath(*class_path_entries)

log.debug("Starting up JVM")
import jnius
from jnius import autoclass

log.debug("JVM has started")


plg_info_cls = autoclass("org.jdownloader.extensions.ExtensionController")
print(plg_info_cls)


```

However, this fails:

```

<snip a bunch of generic logging>

java.lang.IllegalStateException: You cannot init the configinterface interface org.appwork.utils.logging2.LogConfig twice
        at org.appwork.storage.config.handler.StorageHandler.addStorageHandler(StorageHandler.java:438)
        at org.appwork.storage.config.handler.StorageHandler.<init>(StorageHandler.java:331)
        at org.appwork.storage.config.JsonConfig.create(JsonConfig.java:129)
        at org.appwork.utils.logging2.LogSourceProvider.<init>(LogSourceProvider.java:133)
        at org.jdownloader.logging.LogController.<init>(LogController.java:57)
        at org.jdownloader.logging.LogController.<clinit>(LogController.java:26)
        at org.jdownloader.extensions.ExtensionController.<init>(ExtensionController.java:80)
        at org.jdownloader.extensions.ExtensionController.<clinit>(ExtensionController.java:59)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_junshorten.py", line 6, in <module>
    import junshorten
  File "/media/Storage/Scripts/unshortenit/junshorten/__init__.py", line 23, in <module>
    plg_info_cls = autoclass("org.jdownloader.extensions.ExtensionController")
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/jnius/reflect.py", line 158, in autoclass
    c = find_javaclass(clsname)
  File "jnius/jnius_export_func.pxi", line 25, in jnius.find_javaclass (jnius/jnius.c:17415)
jnius.JavaException: Class not found b'org/jdownloader/extensions/ExtensionController'

```

It seems like the single `autoclass()` call is resulting in the constructor of the target class being called multiple times, which breaks in this instance because the relevant class here has global internal state.

What's the proper way to do this? I basically know nothing about java.

issue_title: OAuth1Service should support oauth_callback?
issue_body:
According to the OAuth 1.0 scheme, oauth_callback needs to be passed as a part of the Authorization headers into the request_token endpoint.
https://oauth1.wp-api.org/docs/basics/Auth-Flow.html

It seems like OAuth1Service doesn't support this? Maybe it should!

issue_title: setuptools included in requirements.txt generation
issue_body:
##### Describe your environment

1. MacOS 10.12.6
1. Python 3.6.3
1. pipenv, version 9.0.3

##### Expected result

The lock file should include all the dependencies of the installed package. Usually this isn't an issue because setuptools will already be installed, but it prevents me from using `pip wheel` against the generated requirements to create wheels.

##### Actual result

`setuptools` is excluded from the lock file and requirements which prevents the generation of wheels using `pip wheel`.

##### Steps to replicate

```
$ pipenv install python-json-logger
...
$ pipenv graph
python-json-logger==0.1.8
  - setuptools [required: Any, installed: 38.5.1]
$ grep setuptools Pipfile.lock || echo "not found"
not found
$ pipenv lock --requirements > requirements.txt
$ $ virtualenv .tmp
...
max:test pete$ ./tmp/bin/pip wheel -r requirements.txt
Collecting python-json-logger==0.1.8 (from -r requirements.txt (line 1))
  File was already downloaded /Users/pete/projects/test/python_json_logger-0.1.8-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Collecting setuptools (from python-json-logger==0.1.8->-r requirements.txt (line 1))
In --require-hashes mode, all requirements must have their versions pinned with ==. These do not:
    setuptools from https://pypi.python.org/packages/43/41/033a273f9a25cb63050a390ee8397acbc7eae2159195d85f06f17e7be45a/setuptools-38.5.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl#md5=908b8b5e50bf429e520b2b5fa1b350e5 (from python-json-logger==0.1.8->-r requirements.txt (line 1))
```

issue_title: pipenv can not instanciate virtualenv if directory it is running in starts with "-"
issue_body:

##### Issue description

- It is possible to create directories starting with a dash ("-")
- Though highly unpractical this can occur when using CI systems etc.
- Pipenv fails setting up anything inside a directory of that nature, because parameters are parsed wrong (The directory name is considered a parameter because it starts with a dash)

Pipenv working normally

##### Actual result

```
    sys.exit(cli())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 722, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)

    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 1066, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 535, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/cli.py", line 402, in install
    selective_upgrade=selective_upgrade,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/core.py", line 1932, in do_install
    requirements_dir=requirements_directory,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/pipenv/core.py", line 1369, in do_init
```

##### Steps to replicate

Provide the steps to replicate (which usually at least includes the commands and the Pipfile).

1. create directory starting with a dash
`mkdir -- -foo`
2. enter said directory
`cd -- -foo`
3. start pipenv:
`pipenv install .`

-------------
This is probably not a very high priority for most people but it can lead to broken builds when directories are created automatically;

##### Issue description

in Windows gitbash: 
running pipenv shell
starting a python app inside pipenv shell (I used a simple http app) 
terminating the app with Crtl + C
typing exit + return to exit pipenv shell

issue_title: extras_require behaving differently in setuptools 36.2.1, breaking Twisted test
issue_body:
This Twisted test started failing this morning with setuptools 36.2.1:

https://github.com/twisted/twisted/blob/trunk/src/twisted/python/test/test_setup.py#L76

```
    def test_distributeTakesExtrasRequire(self):
        """
        Setuptools' Distribution object parses and stores its C{extras_require}
        argument as an attribute.
        """
        extras = dict(im_an_extra_dependency="thing")
        attrs = dict(extras_require=extras)
        distribution = Distribution(attrs)
        self.assertEqual(
            extras,
            distribution.extras_require
        )
```

With this:

```
===============================================================================
[FAIL]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/crodrigues/twisted1/src/twisted/python/test/test_setup.py", line 86, in test_distributeTakesExtrasRequire
    distribution.extras_require
  File "/Users/crodrigues/twisted1/src/twisted/trial/_synctest.py", line 432, in assertEqual
    super(_Assertions, self).assertEqual(first, second, msg)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.10_2/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/unittest/case.py", line 515, in assertEqual
    assertion_func(first, second, msg=msg)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.10_2/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/lib/python2.7/unittest/case.py", line 837, in assertDictEqual
    self.fail(self._formatMessage(msg, standardMsg))
twisted.trial.unittest.FailTest: {'im_an_extra_dependency': 'thing'} != {'im_an_extra_dependency': ['thing']}
- {'im_an_extra_dependency': 'thing'}
+ {'im_an_extra_dependency': ['thing']}
?                            +       +


twisted.python.test.test_setup.OptionalDependenciesTests.test_distributeTakesExtrasRequire
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

Looks like the value in the dictionary is now being converted to a list,
whereas before it was not.

issue_title: Regression: sdists no longer install package data correctly with 35.0.0
issue_body:
# Problem description

I have a package that uses `setuptools_scm` and `include_package_data=True` to include some Django templates and static files.

With setuptools 34.4.1, installing an sdist will install these package data files without any problems. However, with setuptools 35.0.0, installing the same sdist file will no longer install these package data files.

Note:

* Only installing sdists seem to be affected by this problem. In particular:
  * _Generating_ sdists seems unaffected: both setuptools 34 and 35 generate sdists with the same (correct) distribution content and `SOURCES.txt`.
  * Installing sdists previously generated with either setuptools version works correctly with 34.4.1, and fails with 35.0.0.
* Wheels seem to be unaffected by this, and build and install fine with both setuptools 34 and 35.

# Cause?

As far as I can tell, this regression is due to #436 / #1014, which no longer reads the sdist package's existing manifest. When the manifest gets regenerated (?), the files that were previously included by `setuptools_scm` while building the sdist are no longer included anymore.

issue_title: Improve user-agent ordering
issue_body:
According to [RFC 7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3), "the product identifiers are listed in decreasing order of their significance for identifying user agent software."

Setuptools instead supplies a user agent like "Python-urllib/3.5 setuptools/21.2.2".

Better would be for setuptools to follow the RFC, placing 'setuptools' first.

issue_title: virtualenv's develop branch fails to create virtualenvs with a different Python interpreter
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. git clone -b develop https://github.com/pypa/virtualenv
2. cd virtualenv
3. python2.7 virtualenv.py -p python3.4 /tmp/py34

The error is
```
Running virtualenv with interpreter /usr/bin/python3.4
Using base prefix '/usr'
New python executable in /tmp/py34/bin/python3.4
Also creating executable in /tmp/py34/bin/python
ERROR: The executable /tmp/py34/bin/python3.4 is not functioning
ERROR: It thinks sys.prefix is '/usr' (should be '/tmp/py34')
ERROR: virtualenv is not compatible with this system or executable
```

I get the same error if I use `-p python2.6`, or `-p python3.3`, or `-p python3.2`, or `-p python3.1`.

I don't get the error if I use `-p python2.7` or `-p pypy`.

I don't get the error if I switch to the `master` branch and use `-p python3.4`.

issue_title: [DNS] Parameter '--record-set-name' should have shortcut '-n'
issue_body:
Adding and removing records is the #1 use case for Azure DNS.

This is done via the various command groups/commands under 'az network dns record ....'

In all of these commands, you have to specify the record set name. Currently, this parameter requires you to type '--record-set-name'. That's a lot of typing. It needs a shortcut.

The only sensible shortcut is '-n' for consistency with the commands under 'az network dns record-set ...'

issue_title: [service] Non-provisioned VM blocks Image deletion; Image doesn't take a lease on the backing blob
issue_body:
I found what look like at least two different service issues today.

Basically, I've got an Image that I can't delete, and whose backing blob no longer exists. Neither of these seem like situations I should be able to get into.

Note: I think some of this is specifically because the VM I booted didn't report successful provisioning to the platform, so Azure thinks the VM is still booting. It's unclear though, why this would block the Image deletion. Presumably at this time, the Image was copied to a new blob for the OS Disk... but maybe there are backend COW optimizations...?

## Repro

1. Create an RG "aznixos"
2. Create a Storage account and container in that RG
3. Upload a VHD to the storage container
4. Make an "Image" from the VHD Blob.
5. Create a new RG "colemick-nixos-20180423"
6. Create a VM using the Image from the other resource group
7. Later, decide you don't want that image anymore.
8. Delete the `aznixos` resource group
---

**Expected**: The resource group is deleted.

**Alternative Expectation**: The resource group is NOT deleted, because the Image took a lease on the backing blob, which would then prevent the 

**Actual**: The RG fails to delete after sometime (an hour?). Manually attempting to delete the Image results in this error: (which is amusing, at this point in time, the backing blob for the Image doesn't exist, so the Image is probably useless anyway)

```
Operation Not Allowed
Operation 'Image Delete' is not allowed since the Virtual Machines 'colemick-nixosdev' are being provisioned using the Image 'nixos-image-16.09.1694.019dcc3-x86_64-linux-vhd'.
```

### Issues

From my deduction, it seems like there might be three issues:

1. Why didn't the Image take a lease out on the blob?
2. Why does the VM block image deletion? It appears that a user can block Image deletion by booting a VM that doesn't complete provisionig.
3. Why does ARM just keep retrying this delete?

While looking into this, I've noticed other oddities:

1. I'd noticed this literally all the time when using Azure, but hte Activity Log is completely unreliable. There was no evidence of my attempt to delete the `aznixos` group in the Activity Log, despite having done in multiple times. I would've assumed the Activity Log accuracy would be important, but it just doesn't seem that way.

2. The resource group just reverted back to "Success!" after the deletion failed. That just doesn't seem quite right.

cc: @yugangw-msft

issue_title: [cliquet] add a Server-Flush header
issue_body:
original: https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/issues/536

@leplatrem suggested:

- The /__flush__ endpoint stores the current timestamp (current time) in the cache backend
- We add a header Server-Flush that gives the value to the client
- With memory backend, we set Server-Flush on server start
- The client obtains this server-flush, if it is superior to _since then it means the server was flushed meanwhile

@natim said he liked the solution

issue_title: Import used by type annotation inside __new__ is considered unused
issue_body:
*Original report by [thauk-copperleaf](https://launchpad.net/~thauk-copperleaf) (@thauk-copperleaf?) on [Launchpad](https://bugs.launchpad.net/bugs/1670468):*

------------------------------------

An import used by a type annotation inside `__new__()` in a metaclass is considered unused by flake8/pyflakes/whatever component.

e.g. given:

    from django.db.models import Model


    class SomeMetaclass(type):
        def __new__(mcs, name, bases, attrs):
            model_class_attrs: [(str, Model,)] = []
            model_class_attrs.clear()
            return super().__new__(mcs, name, bases, attrs)


Running flake8 results in the error:

    example.py:1:1: F401 'django.db.models.Model' imported but unused


Versions:

Python 3.6
flake8 3.3.0
mccabe 0.6.1
pycodestyle 2.3.1
pyflakes 1.5.0

issue_title: Create tutorial for rendering
Idea for easier use of composite generics
Clean up handling of ndarray-like data in TCMPS
issue_body:
Every multidimensional array in TCMPS is passed as four arguments: a float pointer for data, an array size, an int pointer for shape, and size for dimensionality. This leads to function signatures such as:
```
  void Forward(void *_Nonnull ptr, size_t sz, int64_t *_Nonnull shape, int dim,
               void *_Nonnull label_ptr, size_t label_sz, int64_t *_Nonnull label_shape, int label_dim,
               void *_Nonnull weight_ptr, size_t weight_sz, int64_t *_Nonnull weight_shape, int weight_dim,
               bool loss_image_required, bool is_train,
               float *_Nonnull out);
```

An ndarray abstraction at this level would simplify the code, making it more readable and less error-prone. This would also be useful for the CoreML export interface, which currently relies on populating an opaque table:
```
  std::unordered_map<std::string,
                     std::tuple<std::string, float *, int, std::vector<int>>>
      table_;
```

This is also a good opportunity to converge this library towards Objective C, since C++ isn't currently adding much value (given that it wraps a pure Objective-C API and exposes a pure C API to the Python layer).

issue_title: Bounding boxes drawn on images sometimes result in unreadable text in .explore()
issue_body:
Repro steps:

1. Load some large (4k+) images, with bounding box annotations, into an SFrame
2. Draw bounding boxes on the images using `object_detector.util.draw_bounding_boxes`
3. Call `.explore` on the SFrame with images with drawn bounding boxes

Expected: the bounding boxes look crisp, and the text (label and confidence) is readable
Actual: the bounding boxes look fuzzy, and the text is unreadable, due to image scaling to produce the thumbnail and resized hover image in the explore view.

issue_title: Packages with underscore get double entry in `conda list`
issue_body:
When installing a package `foo_bar` into a conda environment, the package comes up in `conda list` as both `foo_bar` and `foo-bar`. 

The bld.bat is standard: 
```
"%PYTHON%" setup.py install
if errorlevel 1 exit 1
```

The setup.py is standard too: 
```
import os
from setuptools import setup, find_packages

pcknm = 'foo_bar'
packages = [pcknm] + [os.path.join(pcknm, sub) for sub in find_packages(pcknm)]
setup(
    name=pcknm,
    version='0.1',
    packages=packages,
)
```

conda list returns something like this
```
foo-bar             0.1                       <pip>
foo_bar               v0.02                    py27_0    file:///c|/Miniconda/conda-bld/win-64/foo_bar-v0.02-py27_0.tar.bz2
```

issue_title: centralized control over .condarc
issue_body:
For Anaconda Server and centralized installations of Anaconda, or for "centralized" installations of conda, without any Python specific pieces, it seems to me that the condarc should either enable loading of other condarc configs, or have a different look up order, e.g.

CLI option (if you really want to override it)
$CONDARC (another way to override)
/etc/condarc (centralized)
sys.prefix + "condarc" (no dot) (deployment specific)
$HOME/.condarc (user specific)
./.condarc (current directory -- possibly risky)

This is motivated by the situation with Wakari where we really need users to keep our default packages and special channels in their condarc.  If they don't, then they will get "standard" packages for things that we've had to patch (in particular, ipython), and new envs won't work with Wakari.  I could imagine this would be a common situation.

issue_title: V2 signature support
issue_body:
DreamHost / DreamObjects supports V4 signatures, but Google Storage in S3-compatible mode doesn't.  The minio-go library supports V4 and V2 signatures to be compatible with Google Cloud Storage, but it seems the Python lib doesn't:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "mintest.py", line 37, in <module>
    for obj in objects:
  File "minio/api.py", line 958, in list_objects
    headers=headers)
  File "minio/api.py", line 1830, in _url_open
    region = self._get_bucket_region(bucket_name)
  File "minio/api.py", line 1764, in _get_bucket_region
    region = self._get_bucket_location(bucket_name)
  File "minio/api.py", line 1802, in _get_bucket_location
    raise response_error.get(bucket_name)
minio.error.ResponseError: ResponseError: code: SignatureDoesNotMatch, message: The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. Check your Google secret key and signing method., bucket_name: xxx, object_name: None, request_id: , host_id: , region: 
```
It would be great to  have Google Storage compatibility in the Python version too.

I looked at the Go version (don't know Go), and it seems to be checking the host name for 'storage.googleapis.com'.   A more general / automatic way (like minio's automatic bucket location detection) might be to start out using V4 signatures.  If a SignatureDoesNotMatch error is thrown while using V4 signatures, set the client for V2 signatures.  If a SignatureDoesNotMatch error occurs while using V2 signatures, fail.  

Or, for my use, I don't mind passing an additional arg when creating the client, like sigversion=2 or 4.  I already have a small config file and can add a new field for this.  Since any future S3-compatible storage will more than likely be V4, maybe this is the easiest way.

issue_title: Python3 - invalid signature, str vs bytes, in sha256, in put_object
issue_body:
In api.py, in function put_object there is a line:

`data_sha256_hex = encode_to_hex(get_sha256(current_data))`

which causes the upload code using put_object fail when running under python 3.5 with invalid signature error. Sha256 hex string contains "r'" and "'" (sic). It should not to make signatures computed and sent match.
The line shall be:

`data_sha256_hex = encode_to_hex(get_sha256(current_data)).decode('ascii')`

As I went with other solution to my problems, I don't have time to fully fix this issue (as it seems that in some places bytes are used; in some - strings - I'd has to be a more systematic fix to support python3 IMHO).

issue_title: Self looping not allowed and other queries
issue_body:
### Subject of the issue
Thank you for this amazing library. It is very useful. I am trying to use pgmpy to generate markov and hidden markov models. I have tried using MarkovModel() to create a basic markov model with three nodes and I want to add a self-loop in a node.

### Your environment
* pgmpy version: latest
* Python version: python 3
* Operating System: mac OS

### Steps to reproduce
student = MarkovModel([('Alice', 'Bob'), ('Alice', 'Alice'), ('Bob', 'Charles')])

### Expected behaviour
Successfully create a markov model.

### Actual behaviour
ValueError: Self loops are not allowed.

### Additional queries

issue_title: full_view_item broken for custom dexterity types in plone 5
issue_body:
# User Problem

Custom dexterity content types break the `All content` view of a container. 

## Steps to reproduce problem

1. Create a dexterity content item and add some fields
2. Add the created item to a container
3. Set the container to 'all content' display

## Further information

The item macro for the content item is called in [p.a.contenttypes/browser/templates/full_view_item.pt](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/blob/master/plone/app/contenttypes/browser/templates/full_view_item.pt#L47)

Dexterity uses [plone.autoform.view.WidgetsView](https://github.com/plone/plone.autoform/blob/master/plone/autoform/view.py#L16) in [DefaultView](https://github.com/plone/plone.dexterity/blob/master/plone/dexterity/browser/view.py#L8)  but the [class](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/blob/master/plone/app/contenttypes/browser/configure.zcml#L144) for `@@full_view_item` is [FullViewItem](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.contenttypes/blob/master/plone/app/contenttypes/browser/full_view.py#L7)

Strangely enough, if I use `<?python import pdb; pdb.set_trace() ?>` in full_view_item.pt and then in the debugger call `econtext['view'].update()` the form gets set up and renders.

So I'm not sure how this all ties together, or whether it will be possible to use the all content view with custom dexterity types. Any pointers, or discussion on a way to resolve the issue would be appreciated.

issue_title: `rdf:parseType` shouldn't be converted into `rdf:datatype`
issue_body:
When parsing a file that has an object with `rdf:parseType`, it is automatically converted into `rdf:datatype`.  `rdf:parseTypes` should be handled explicity instead of converted and they change the xml output:

```xml
<dcterms:description rdf:parseType="Literal">This is a simple description</dcterms:description>
```
gets changed to:

```xml
<dcterms:description rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#XMLLiteral">This is a simple description</dcterms:description>
```

issue_title: RDF/XML behaves weird if RDF is the default namespace
issue_body:
Noticed this while trying to parse mozilla addon install.rdf files. They use `xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"` so they don't have to prefix things like `<Description>` or `about` with `<rdf:Description>` or `rdf:about`.

Parsing such a file the current rdflib will not understand the rdf basics anymore and introduce BNodes to capture the xml.

Examples:

Here we define the rdf namespace as commonly done:
```python
In [64]: my_data = '''
<rdf:RDF
  xmlns:rdf='http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#'
  xmlns:rdfs='http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#'
>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="urn:mozilla:install-manifest">
    <rdfs:label>Example</rdfs:label>
    <rdfs:comment>This is really just an example.</rdfs:comment>
  </rdf:Description>
</rdf:RDF>
'''

In [65]: g = rdflib.Graph()

In [66]: g.parse(data=my_data)
Out[66]: <Graph identifier=N84e0685a0c7c4dd2be6a59ad9cbadac9 (<class 'rdflib.graph.Graph'>)>

In [67]: list(g)
Out[67]:
[(rdflib.term.URIRef(u'urn:mozilla:install-manifest'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'Example')),
 (rdflib.term.URIRef(u'urn:mozilla:install-manifest'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'This is really just an example.'))]
```

this is ok.

let's use something other than rdf, e.g. fdr:
```python
In [76]: my_data = '''
   ....: <fdr:RDF
   ....:   xmlns:fdr='http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#'
   ....:   xmlns:rdfs='http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#'
   ....: >
   ....:   <fdr:Description fdr:about="urn:mozilla:install-manifest">
   ....:     <rdfs:label>Example</rdfs:label>
   ....:     <rdfs:comment>This is really just an example.</rdfs:comment>
   ....:   </fdr:Description>
   ....: </fdr:RDF>
   ....: '''

In [77]: g = rdflib.Graph()

In [78]: g.parse(data=my_data)
Out[78]: <Graph identifier=N877052e9ac414a52841a8157ab590fb2 (<class 'rdflib.graph.Graph'>)>

In [79]: list(g)
Out[79]:
[(rdflib.term.URIRef(u'urn:mozilla:install-manifest'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'Example')),
 (rdflib.term.URIRef(u'urn:mozilla:install-manifest'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'This is really just an example.'))]
```
this looks ok as well.

now let's make rdf the default namespace so we don't need to prefix everything with rdf (2nd line):
```python
In [68]: my_data = '''
<RDF
  xmlns='http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#'
  xmlns:rdfs='http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#'
>
  <Description about="urn:mozilla:install-manifest">
    <rdfs:label>Example</rdfs:label>
    <rdfs:comment>This is really just an example.</rdfs:comment>
  </Description>
</RDF>
'''

In [69]: g = rdflib.Graph()

In [70]: g.parse(data=my_data)
Out[70]: <Graph identifier=Nc29cd64640354624874a897aea509b12 (<class 'rdflib.graph.Graph'>)>

In [71]: list(g)
Out[71]:
[(rdflib.term.BNode('N6da6e3fa36bc4a898e3ef99e67e29cc7'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'about'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'urn:mozilla:install-manifest')),
 (rdflib.term.BNode('N6da6e3fa36bc4a898e3ef99e67e29cc7'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'Example')),
 (rdflib.term.BNode('N6da6e3fa36bc4a898e3ef99e67e29cc7'),
  rdflib.term.URIRef(u'http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment'),
  rdflib.term.Literal(u'This is really just an example.'))]
```

this looks as if rdflib doesn't understand rdf anymore, as it introduces a BNode above all :(

issue_title: Literal.__str__ does not behave like unicode
issue_body:
pierre.a...@gmail.com, 2010-05-18T13:29:40.000Z 
Literal is a subclass of unicode.
However, its __str__ method has not the same semantics as unicode's,
because it uses the "unicode-escape" codec instead of the default system 
codec.

>>> from rdflib import Literal
>>> str(unicode('a\n'))
'a\n'
>>> str(Literal('a\n'))
'a\\n'
>>> str(unicode(Literal('a\n')))
'a\n'

This might be a feature, but breaks expectations: I tend to use Literals as 
any other unicode strings (slicing, startswith, etc...), so having to 
manage them specially only for str conversion seems a misfeature.

Comment 1 by eike...@gmail.com
Pierre, thank you for reporting this issue.

I know there are some unresolvable issues that arise from the choice of having Literal subclass unicode. I'm not 
sure if this is one of the unresolvable ones or not. We should take a look to see if we can fix this issue without 
breaking other bits. I'll work on a patch now to see what all fixing this will entail.


Comment 2 by eik...@gmail.com
This issue was closed by revision r1876.


Comment 3 by eike...@gmail.com
Unless this breaks bits for which we do not yet have a test case. We are good to go; the fix was fairly straight 
forward. If there are broken bits as a result of this change we'll need a test case for it.


Comment 4 by eik...@gmail.com
This issue was closed by revision a37a1cf8a191.

```pytb
TypeError: initializer for ctype 'char' must be a bytes of length 1, not int
```

This is on python 3.5. I'm not sure whether this should be reported here or in cryptography.

Looks like the problem might be in RawRegistrationResponse._fixsig. It calls list() on a bytestring to convert it to a list of integers, but never converts the list back to a bytestring. This patch does "fix" the issue, but I don't know if it's the correct solution and probably doesn't work on python 2:

```patch
diff --git a/u2flib_server/u2f_v2.py b/u2flib_server/u2f_v2.py
index 52793bf..3851871 100644
--- a/u2flib_server/u2f_v2.py
+++ b/u2flib_server/u2f_v2.py
@@ -107,8 +107,8 @@ class RawRegistrationResponse(object):
 
         if subject in FIXSIG:  # Set unused bits in signature to 0
             der = list(cert.public_bytes(Encoding.DER))
-            der[-257] = b'\x00'
-            cert = certificate_from_der(der)
+            der[-257] = 0
+            cert = certificate_from_der(bytes(der))
         return cert
 
     def serialize(self):
```

issue_title: Infer shape partial returns () after slice / slice_axis operations
issue_body:
Code snippet

```
var1 = mx.sym.var(name="data", shape=(0,20))
var2 = mx.sym.slice(var1,begin=(None, None), end=(None,10))
var2.infer_shape_partial()

# Actual Output : ([(0, 20)], [()], [])

# Expected Output: ([(0, 20)], [(0,10)], [])
```
Same behavior for `slice_axis` and `slice` operators.
To summarize: Even if one dimension is 0 (i.e., unknown), infer shape partial returns empty tuple.

However, note that if you use other operators like broadcast_add() broadcast_sub() etc. will return expected output on infer_shape_partial().

Per @reminisce  - This is a buggy behavior in few operators. https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/src/operator/tensor/matrix_op-inl.h#L693 => This makes it return early than try to infer partial shapes.

This is a blocking issue in keras-mxnet for users who use slice operator and custom loss functionality.

@rahul003

issue_title: [Question] Use LSTM for Sine Wave prediction
issue_body:
I was planning to learn mxnet and also try to contribute to some simple examples. I posted below question on stackoverflow but unfortunately no response got.  So I would try here:
Below is the code I wrote trying to reproduce the Tensorflow example.  (location at https://github.com/mouradmourafiq/tensorflow-lstm-regression/blob/master/lstm_sin.ipynb" 

```
import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import argparse
import os
import sys
from data_processing import generate_data
import logging
head = '%(asctime)-15s %(message)s'
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG, format=head)
TIMESTEPS = 3
BATCH_SIZE = 100
X, y = generate_data(np.sin, np.linspace(0, 100, 10000), TIMESTEPS, seperate=False)
train_iter = mx.io.NDArrayIter(X['train'], y['train'], batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, label_name='lro_label')
eval_iter = mx.io.NDArrayIter(X['val'], y['val'], batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False)
test_iter = mx.io.NDArrayIter(X['test'], batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False)
num_layers = 3
num_hidden = 50

data = mx.sym.Variable('data')
label = mx.sym.Variable('lro_label')

stack = mx.rnn.SequentialRNNCell()
for i in range(num_layers):
    stack.add(mx.rnn.LSTMCell(num_hidden=num_hidden, prefix='lstm_l%d_'%i))
#stack.reset()
outputs, states = stack.unroll(length=TIMESTEPS,
                               inputs=data,
                               layout='NTC',
                               merge_outputs=True)

outputs = mx.sym.reshape(outputs, shape=(BATCH_SIZE, -1))
# purpose of fc1 was to make shape change to (batch_size, *), or label shape won't match LSTM unrolled output shape.
outputs = mx.sym.FullyConnected(data=outputs, num_hidden=1, name='fc1')
label = mx.sym.reshape(label, shape=(-1,))
outputs = mx.sym.LinearRegressionOutput(data=outputs, 
                               label=label,
                               name='lro')
contexts = mx.cpu(0)
model = mx.mod.Module(symbol = outputs,
                     data_names = ['data'],
                     label_names = ['lro_label'])
model.fit(train_iter, eval_iter,
         optimizer_params = {'learning_rate':0.005},
         num_epoch=4,
         batch_end_callback=mx.callback.Speedometer(BATCH_SIZE, 2))
```

This code runs but the train_accuracy is always Nan and validation accuracy always 1. The question is how to make it correct? 
And since unrolled out shape has sequence_length, how can it match to label shape? Does my FC1 net make sense?

issue_title: swarm services: extra_hosts set in wrong format
issue_body:
Dear all,
we encounter issues with setting `extra_hosts` for swarm services on docker-py 2.6.1

```
docker version
Client:
 Version:      17.06.2-ce
 API version:  1.30
 Go version:   go1.8.3
 Git commit:   cec0b72
 Built:        Tue Sep  5 19:59:06 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:
 Version:      17.06.2-ce
 API version:  1.30 (minimum version 1.12)
 Go version:   go1.8.3
 Git commit:   cec0b72
 Built:        Tue Sep  5 20:00:25 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
 Experimental: false
```
**What we do**
```
extra_hosts = dict()
extra_hosts.update({'domain.com': '8.8.8.8'})
client.services.create(name='myservice', image='nginx', hosts=extra_hosts)
```

**Result**
The entry is not found in `/etc/hosts` in the container.
The attribute `Hosts` of the service's `ContainerSpec` shows the following syntax: `domain.com:8.8.8.8`
However, the syntax should have been `8.8.8.8 domain.com`

**Analysis**
docker-py uses this line to format `extra_hosts` (https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/2.6.1/docker/utils/utils.py#L569):
```
 '{}:{}'.format(k, v) for k, v in sorted(six.iteritems(extra_hosts))
```

According to swarm's API (https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/d91c5f42eb37c6f88cec4021c10c0a1ded1785c3/api/types/swarm/container.go#L65), the ContainerSpec of a TaskSpec expects `extra_hosts` in the format described per http://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/hosts.5.html which is:
```
IP HostnameA
```

**Workaround**
Currently, we compensate this as follows:
```
create_service_kwargs = docker.models.services._get_create_service_kwargs(...)
create_service_kwargs['task_template']['ContainerSpec']['Hosts'] = ['8.8.8.8 domain.com']
client.api.create_service(**create_service_kwargs)
```

Maybe you can comment on this.

Thanks and Regards,
Stephen

issue_title: IL Linker issue with asp.net core
issue_body:
I've followed the instructions on how to apply the mono IL linker to a .NET core 2.0 application (an ASP.NET core project), but I'm getting runtime exceptions telling me there's no parameterless constructor for one of my model classes

**_TL;DR_**
I can't make the IL linker include the parameterless constructor of one of my model classes in my ASP.NET Core 2.0 app, even if I specifically include it as root. Is it my fault or the linker's?

This is the exception trace:

```Unhandled Exception: System.MissingMethodException: No parameterless constructor defined for this object.
   at System.RuntimeTypeHandle.CreateInstance(RuntimeType type, Boolean publicOnly, Boolean& canBeCached, RuntimeMethodHandleInternal& ctor)
   at System.RuntimeType.CreateInstanceSlow(Boolean publicOnly, Boolean skipCheckThis, Boolean fillCache, StackCrawlMark& stackMark)
   at System.Activator.CreateInstance(Type type, Boolean nonPublic)
   at System.Activator.CreateInstance(Type type)
   at System.ComponentModel.ReflectTypeDescriptionProvider.SearchIntrinsicTable(Hashtable table, Type callingType)
   at System.ComponentModel.ReflectTypeDescriptionProvider.ReflectedTypeData.GetConverter(Object instance)
   at System.ComponentModel.TypeDescriptor.TypeDescriptionNode.DefaultTypeDescriptor.System.ComponentModel.ICustomTypeDescriptor.GetConverter()
   at System.ComponentModel.TypeDescriptor.GetConverter(Type type)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.TryConvertValue(Type type, String value, Object& result, Exception& error)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.BindInstance(Type type, Object instance, IConfiguration config)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.BindProperty(PropertyInfo property, Object instance, IConfiguration config)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.BindNonScalar(IConfiguration configuration, Object instance)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.BindInstance(Type type, Object instance, IConfiguration config)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.ConfigurationBinder.Bind(IConfiguration configuration, Object instance)
   at tts2.Startup.ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.ConventionBasedStartup.ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.EnsureApplicationServices()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Internal.WebHost.BuildApplication()
   at Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.WebHostBuilder.Build()
   at tts2.Program.Main(String[] args)
```

The exception is triggered here (only relevant code):
```c#
namespace tts2
{
    public class Program()
    {
        public static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            WebHost.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
                .UseStartup<Startup>()
                .Build();
        }
    }

    public class Startup
    {
        public IConfiguration Configuration { get; }

        public Startup(IConfiguration configuration)
        {
            Configuration = configuration;
        }

        public void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
        {
            // stuff

            // The following line throws the exception when published with the IL linker
            MicrosoftLoginSettings loginSettings = new MicrosoftLoginSettings();
            Configuration.GetSection("MicrosoftLoginSettings").Bind(loginSettings);

            // stuff
        }
    }
}

namespace Tts.Settings
{
    public class MicrosoftLoginSettings
    {
        public MicrosoftLoginSettings()
        {
        }

        public string ClientId { get; set; }
        public string ClientSecret { get; set; }
    }
}
```

I added the linker step as required. My nuget.config file:
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<configuration>
 <packageSources>
  <add key="dotnet-core" value="https://dotnet.myget.org/F/dotnet-core/api/v3/index.json" />
 </packageSources>
</configuration>
```

And my tts2.csproj file:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>Tts</RootNamespace>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="ILLink.Tasks" Version="0.1.4-preview-981901" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.All" Version="2.0.0" />
    <!-- more PackageReferences... -->
  </ItemGroup>
 <!-- more ItemGroups... -->
</Project>
```


I'm guessing this has something to do with the linker not seeing through reflection, as expected. However, I decided to try the advanced options and explicitly set the assembly as root. This is what I added in my tts2.csproj:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <!-- stuff -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <LinkerRootAssemblies Include="tts2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

That didn't change the outcome, so I tried targeting the MicrosoftLoginSettings type specifically. This is what my tts2.csproj looked like then:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <!-- stuff -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <LinkerRootDescriptors Include="linker.xml" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And this is my `linker.xml` file:
```xml
<linker>
  <assembly fullname="tts2">
    <type fullname="Tts.Settings.MicrosoftLoginSettings" required="false" />
  </assembly>
</linker>
```

I also tried without the `required="false"` attribute: same result.

It seems that nothing I do prevents the linker from removing that parameterless constructor. I'm not quite sure I'm doing the right thing, so please feel free to correct me if anything above isn't right!

issue_title: Services from `/arlas/explore/*` accepting a filter in the `GET` or `POST` should also accept a filter in the `HEADER`
issue_body:
In gitlab by @sylvaingaudan on Aug 1, 2017, 11:57

Services from `/arlas/explore/*` accepting a filter in the `GET` or `POST` should also accept a filter in the `HEADER`.
The filter is specified in the JSON format and a `AND` operator is applied between the filter provided in the GET/POST and the filter provided in the `HEADER`.

Note: In some cases, the filter is currently appended as `OR`, if for example two `equal` operators are applied on a same field. This behavior is not acceptable for the present case : a real  `AND` operator must be applied.

Reference for DeepMind using FireResetEnv wrapper's functionality
issue_body:
In [atari_wrappers.py](https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/master/baselines/common/atari_wrappers.py) (specifically, the wrap_deepmind function all the way at the bottom), the docstring says that that function configures the environment for "DeepMind-style Atari".

I was curious if anyone can provide me with a reference to any DeepMind paper mentioning that they do in fact use functionality as implemented by the FireResetEnv wrapper? I was able to find mentions of the functionality implemented by all the other wrappers applied in that function (and also the ones in the make_atari function above) in various DeepMind papers (such as the Mnih et al. (2015) DQN Nature paper), but was unable to find any text resembling the functionality of the FireResetEnv.

It may just be a minor detail, but I do think it's important to be precise with this kind of stuff for the sake of reproducibility.

issue_title: policies of PPO2 contains relu as activation function in pi and vf
issue_body: In ppo2/policies.py, the output of network, `pi = fc(h5, 'pi', nact)`, and `vf = fc(h5, 'v', 1)` utilize relu as activation function in default. It's ok if value function is always greater than zero but for negative value functions,the output is always zero. issue_title: Provide the sources of the documentation in the source distribution instead of a build
issue_body:
Similar to #31 but for the documentation. Again, please let the build system build the documentation. Sphinx is still evolving and produce different files as a result from one system to another.

For Debian in particular, some of the javascript code may need to be patched for security reasons if the documentation is produced using an old version of sphinx. As a result, it is advised to provide the source instead of a build and let the packaging system handle the build and potential patching.

In terms of changes, it should be just a matter of modifying the `MANIFEST.in` file and regenerate + upload the source distribution with `python setup.py register sdist upload`.

issue_title: Compiling two modules with different compilers leads to segfault in class.h
issue_body:
We have a library that uses pybind11 to wrap its internal C++ code. We now also want to allow external extension modules to be usable with the library. However, we are noticing that when our library is built with one compiler and an extension module with another, there is a segfault within pybind11 upon import.

I am able to reproduce the bug with a small example:

`a.cpp` (imagine our library)
```c++
#include <pybind11/pybind11.h>

namespace py = pybind11;

struct A {
  explicit A(int y) : _y(y) {}
  int f(int x) { return x + _y; }
  int _y;
};

PYBIND11_MODULE(a, m) {
  py::class_<A>(m, "A").def(py::init<int>()).def("f", &A::f);
}
```

`b.cpp` (imagine an extension)
```c++
#include <pybind11/pybind11.h>

namespace py = pybind11;

struct B {
  explicit B(int y) : _y(y) {}
  int f(int x) { return x + _y; }
  int _y;
};

PYBIND11_MODULE(b, m) {
  py::class_<B>(m, "B").def(py::init<int>()).def("f", &B::f);
}
```

`setup_a.py`
```python
from setuptools import setup, Extension
from setuptools.command.build_ext import build_ext

ext_modules = [
    Extension('a', ['a.cpp'], include_dirs=['../include'], language='c++'),
]


class BuildExtension(build_ext):
    """A custom build extension for adding compiler-specific options."""

    def build_extensions(self):
        for extension in self.extensions:
            extension.extra_compile_args = ['-g', '-std=c++11']
        build_ext.build_extensions(self)


setup(
    name='a', ext_modules=ext_modules, cmdclass={
        'build_ext': BuildExtension
    })
```

`setup_b.py`
```python
from setuptools import setup, Extension
from setuptools.command.build_ext import build_ext

ext_modules = [
    Extension('b', ['b.cpp'], include_dirs=['../include'], language='c++'),
]


class BuildExtension(build_ext):
    """A custom build extension for adding compiler-specific options."""

    def build_extensions(self):
        for extension in self.extensions:
            extension.extra_compile_args = ['-g', '-std=c++11']

setup(
    name='b',
    ext_modules=ext_modules,
    cmdclass={
        'build_ext': BuildExtension
```

issue_title: numpy.ndarray onwnership management
issue_body:
Hello, Wenzel!
I have a question on creating numpy.ndarray using existing data buffer pointer.
As the code in numpy.h goes
`      
 if (info.ptr && tmp)
tmp = object(api.PyArray_NewCopy_(tmp.ptr(), -1 /* any order */), false);`
even though we supply pointer to an existing buffer, we create an array and return **a copy** of it thus providing a new ndarray. 
Do we follow this path to ensure proper memory management in terms of array data ownership?(thus ensuring that if data buffer is altered from C++, created ndarray stays intact as there is no way to pass information about such a change to the array)

I can't think of a secure way of passing data pointer to ndarray when we are able to manage the object both from C++ and Python.
My question has been motivated by my attempts to find a way of creating ndarray from existing data with no shared ownership violations.

issue_title: Please help, passing STL structures into py::arg.
dmatrices: fix "ValueError: For numerical factors, num_columns must be an int"
issue_body:
With an interaction term, dmatrices is failing with the error `ValueError: For numerical factors, num_columns must be an int`.  

The tests `if not isinstance(num_columns, int)` in the `design_info` module (lines 87 and 218) return `True` because `num_columns` is a `long` rather than an `int`.  

Changing the test to `if not isinstance(num_columns, (int, long))` has worked for me.

issue_title: Support for nanoseconds
issue_body: We can store nanoseconds in the hdf5 files, when we try to query them, we get error messages of unsupported type. *PyTables*, the framework we use for handling hdf5 files, supports *storing*  the timestamps with nanoseconds. However, *numexpr*, a package used by *PyTables*  when querying the data, *does not*  yet support nanoseconds.

issue_title: An --kernel option for %preview
issue_body: Right now preview can only preview variables in the sos kernel, it would be good to be able to preview variables in another kernel.

issue_title: Name conflict between file and variable for %preview magic.
issue_body:
```
%preview data
```
can fail if there is a directory named `data`. The reason behind this is that `sos` first tries to see if `data` is a valid file or directory name, and try to preview it, and it fails when it is a directory. sos did not continue to check if there is a variable named `data`.

Although name conflict is rare, I think we should try both: try to preview both file `data` and variable `data`, instead of fail after file or directory `data` cannot be previewed.

issue_title: Docker image does not honor `work_dir`?
issue_body:
Not sure if this can be confirmed, but in my run of `STAR` via docker:

```
[align_1 (STAR prefiltering alignment)]

# Followd by samtools remove reads with quality less than {qual_cutoff}
parameter: qual_cutoff = 10
```

The above workflow should supposedly execute under `work_dir`. But from `docker` command output prompt it is not the case. And indeed files are generated to where I run the workflow, not my specified `cwd`.

issue_title: Check Customization status on Windows VM
issue_body:
I'm cloning Linux and Windows VMs from a template, using customization to add NIC, change IP, Hostname, ...
The deployment itself is working fine.
In my workflow I try to check if the new VM is ready to use.
As there is a customization running on it it can take a while.
My current method involves checking the VMs guest obj, and get the ip value.
So far this was working great with the Linux VMs, as they had no IP assigned during the customization run, and network was only started after the final reboot.
So checking the IP was reliable in this case to be sure that the system was up and running, and user can proceed logging in.
However the situation is different with Windows VMs.
It looks like even during the customization run they get an IP.
So my check will trigger this, and continues the workflow.
But this fails, as there will be a reboot or more involved, as customization is still running.

So to come to my actual query, is there a way I can get the status of the host customization from the VM obj, or any other obj?

This is occurring when integrated with Django with the following configuration:


From reading the [documentation](http://pypi.datadoghq.com/trace/docs/#module-ddtrace.contrib.django) which states:
> ENABLED (default: not django_settings.DEBUG): defines if the tracer is enabled or not. If set to false, the code is still instrumented but no spans are sent to the trace agent. It seems this log should not occur. If no spans are sent to the trace agent then presumably a connection should not be established?

issue_title: Why does Lasagne use a "Lazy" construction architecture ?
issue_body:
Lasagne doesn't work as I thought it would. 
The network is generated during the call to lasagne.layers.get_output().
Instinctively I would have constructed the layer theano symbolic output upon call of the layer constructor. 
What is the main use case for the lazy construction of the network ?

My main issues with regards to the lazy approach Lasagne use : 
-requirement to only do one get_output to share the same random numbers 
-It is that it's hard to have multiple outputs, it's hard to have multiple inputs, it is hard to do some things which should be one-liners (like an elemwise product layer). 
-There is also the problem that it's hard to reuse existing layers to create new layers (for example to create a "highway network" (a simple operation on the result from 2 dense networks) it would have been useful to reuse the dense networks. 
-Harder to debug because it sometime crashes on the get_output line for an error which happens earlier.
-Lazy evaluation usually needs harder treatment to avoid things being computed twice (a proper graph construction and traversal which is what theano already do so why redo it again in Lasagne)
-I can't yet thought of a good use case for which the lazy construction of the network would be better than it's immediate construction.

issue_title: Batch normalization in ReLU
issue_body:
Hi,

lasagne.layers.batch_norm conveniently removes the bias term from the layer, as it is redundant with the batchnorms mean subtraction.

However, I recently read [here](http://arxiv.org/abs/1507.02672) (page 6), that in a similar way, for ReLUs the scaling in the Batchnorm is redundant:

> For example, the rectified linear unit does not need scaling, the linear activation function needs neither scaling nor bias, but softmax requires both.

First, I'm not totally sure if its true ( they have a funny nomenclature, and according to their formulas, they use scaling/bias slightly differently). I tried to do the math myself, but couldn't definitely figure out if its correct. Intuitively, it makes sense: You shift your input to zero mean; everything <0 gets squashed to 0 by the RelU; all >0 gets just the identity, and its irrelevant how you scale this.

Can we handle/remove this redundany somehow in batch_norm? Since the ReLU is the default nonlinearity for most (?) layers, this might change quite a bit

issue_title: lasagne.layers.get_output( l_emb ) for an EmbeddingLayer using theano.function( [l_in.input_var], output) raises error
issue_body:
Using l_in.input_var as the input to theano.function() for an EmbeddingLayer per this simple example:

```
import numpy as np
import theano
import lasagne

VOCABULARY_SIZE = 5 
EMBEDDING_SIZE = 10
N_BATCH = 10
MAX_LENGTH = 3 
 
# Prepare data
x_train_set = np.asarray( [[1, 2, 4] for i in xrange(20)], dtype='int32' ) 

# Layers
l_in = lasagne.layers.InputLayer( (N_BATCH, MAX_LENGTH) ) 

l_emb = lasagne.layers.EmbeddingLayer(
  l_in,
  input_size=VOCABULARY_SIZE,
  output_size=EMBEDDING_SIZE,
  W=lasagne.init.Uniform(0.1)
)
```

issue_title: Support for updates
issue_body:
A long-standing issue is the ability to update documents. It's only partially implemented and completely undocumented. As a workaround, users can always:

```python
for name, doc in stream:
    # make edits and, critically, generate new uids
    db.insert(name, doc)
```

issue_title: Implement a 'watch' method that yields new documents by polling
issue_body:
Recall the [zmq-based subscribe PR in bluesky](https://github.com/NSLS-II/bluesky/pull/395) (not to be confused with the zmq-based "RunEngine as a service"). The RunEngine pushes documents to a central hub which (caches them and) pushes them to consumers over the network:

```python
for name, doc in subscribe(host):
    do_stuff(name, doc)
```

As a complement to this (as discussed offline), we need:

```python
for name, doc in db.watch(optional_query_to_narrow_results):
    do_stuff(name, doc)
```

This addresses one of @klauer's concerns with https://github.com/NSLS-II/bluesky/pull/395 : what happens if the user forgets to configure the zmq stuff until after the scan has started? I am personally against a hybrid solution that was once discussed: to pull from databroker to "catch up" and then subscribe to pushes from zmq. But I think having a puller is a good idea, and if someone wanted to build a smart hybrid from these two pieces, they could.

Details to be worked out:
* What happens if `db` is receiving documents from more than one RunEngine at a time? Maybe the RunEngine should insert its hostname + process number + Python ojbect id into the RunStart so that the search query can watch RunEngine instance at a time, if needed.
* How frequent to do the polling? Proposal: multiply polling interval 2 if you don't see a new document on the first try; divide by 2 if you do.

issue_title: Simplify region base classes
issue_body:
In December 2016 @joleroi @astrofrog and I had a Google hangout where we discussed `astropy.regions`, the minutes are here:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u-WjsP4wLWx4g1-om7imstjngOmwpAJQ8EyVZxvhxV4/edit#heading=h.wf1hdss3eefb

I dropped the ball on implementing the changes discussed there, but I'd like to come back to that now. Actually, if this is non-controversial, I'd like to make the changes this week, before the regions 0.2 release, because it's not a lot of work, just removing a bunch of stuff instead of leaving dummy methods that return `NotImplementedError` at the moment.

Before going ahead, I'd like to ask @astrofrog and @joleroi again, and also @keflavich and @larrybradley for input!

---

Basically we said that `area` and `contains` should be removed from the base sky region class:
http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.SkyRegion.html

The reason is that `area` and `contains` aren't well-defined concepts for all sky regions.
We argued about this for quite a while, but in the end I think there was agreement that it's not well-defined and thus doesn't belong in the base class.
E.g. how exactly would you implement `area` and `contains` for
http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.RectangleSkyRegion.html
so that it's valid for very large rectangles (say width = 100 deg)?

So we'd like to propose basically that we adopt the DS9 model of having regions mainly defined as pixel regions, and having sky regions mostly as convenience, but not with separate definitions of `contains` methods. Instead `contains` would be implemented in the following generic way on the base class for sky regions, and only be well-defined if the user has a WCS:

```
class SkyRegion(object):
    def contains(self, sky_coord, wcs):
         Reg = self.to_pixel(wcs)
         Pix_coord = foo(sky_coord)
         Return reg.contains(pix_coord)
```

In the future, we would add the special cases (circle & polygon) back where contains is well-defined in sky coordinates, either as separate classes or on the existing classes.

This brings us to the other major change we'd like to propose, which is to get rid of the different modes in the pixel to sky transformation:
- http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.SkyRegion.html#regions.SkyRegion.to_pixel
- http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.PixelRegion.html#regions.PixelRegion.to_sky

This was never implemented, it was just an idea, there will be no functionality lost.

So for now, we'd remove the modes from the base class, only leaving the "mode" how DS9 transforms back and forth, and we'd remove all the checks and NotImplementedError from the derived classes.

---

Note that `regions` will remain experimental, and based on user and dev feedback, we can always do it differently in the end. So basically -- if there's agreement on this I would make the change tomorrow, before the 0.2 release. And if it's controversial, I would just make the release as-is, and we take our time to discuss here.

issue_title: Improvements to edit_on_github extension
issue_body:
*Originally requested by @embray in https://github.com/astropy/astropy-helpers/issues/211*

In https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/4361#issuecomment-167673551 I suggested a few fixes to the edit_on_github Sphinx extension that might be worth making:
- [ ] If the `doctree` argument passed to `html_page_content` is `None`, this implies an HTML page that was not generated from an actual rst document.  Usually this implies that there is no page to edit on GitHub, though I suppose a static HTML page that lives in the repository is also possible.  In either case, the case of `doctree is None` should be handled in some appropriate way.  Currently if `doctree is None` for a page that is _not_ skipped, the extension crashes.
- [ ] The default for `edit_on_github_skip_regex` should be updated, probably to include `api.*` pages for compatibility with automodapi.  Better yet, maybe the extension can check if automodapi is being used, and automatically skip anything that starts with the value for the `automodapi_toctreedirnm` config option used by automodapi.

issue_title: Speed up travis tests (split out integration tests?)
issue_body:
Travis latency is a pretty major drag on Certbot developers.  Here are some steps could help:
- Get rid of `py27`, since `cover` covers it
- Split integration tests out of the unit test matrix entries, which would allow them to be run in parallel
- Pick some things that we don't expect to fail more than once in blue moon, and only run those during releases and/or on `obs7`.  The most likely candidate would be the `-oldest` matrix elements.

issue_title: Check unchecked types in certbot-dns-google
issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
This issue is for checking untyped definitions in the `certbot-dns-google` module.

To start, add the following to `mypy.ini` in the root of the repo:
```
[mypy-certbot_dns_google.*]
check_untyped_defs = True
```

Then run `tox -e mypy`. If passes, submit a PR. If not, fix errors and submit a PR when tests pass. You can read more about how all of this works below.

See explanation here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fp0tPP9bzYkUViTnOXAyoT_KfgTfs8HSpNMoU8Tm_SY/edit?usp=sharing

From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Add the `--check-untyped-defs` option to your mypy arguments, and get that running with no errors on the codebase. This option causes mypy to check every def in the codebase for internal consistency; mypy can detect many classes of bugs and mistakes in your codebase, without your having written a single type annotation!

In most cases, you’ll want to fix bugs and bad code as you go, but you can also use #type: ignore annotations or exclude files to defer issues. For example, we excluded all of Zulip’s tests at first, since they’re both lower value to type-check and had most of the monkey-patching and other questionable Python in the project. For Zulip, getting clean `--check-untyped-defs` output took me about 2 days of hard work, including merging fixes for about 40 issues in the Zulip codebase.

I spent another day or two generating nice reproducers for the mypy bugs I’d encountered and improving typeshed. Now that mypy is no longer in its infancy, mypy bugs are increasingly rare. But a large project should expect to encounter and fix typeshed issues (just submit a PR!).

Once `mypy --check-untyped-defs` passes on your codebase, you should lock down your progress by running the mypy checker in your CI environment.

Because mypy type annotations are optional, once you’ve done the setup above, you can start annotating your codebase at whatever pace you like. Over time, you’ll get the benefits of static types in the parts of the codebase that you’ve annotated, without needing to do anything special to the rest of the codebase (the wonders of gradual typing!). In the next section, I’ll discuss strategy for how to move the codebase towards being fully annotated.

1. `certbot --nginx -d example.com`
2. `certbot delete --cert-name example.com`.
3. `service nginx reload` (fails)

Expected result:

Certbot errors out at (2) and tells you to remove the certificate from config files before deleting it.

This is a particularly frustrating outcome because in most cases Certbot allows you to get by without editing server config files, but in this case your server will be broken until you edit the config files. And if you've only ever edited SSL/TLS configuration with Certbot, you may not know what you need to fix.

issue_body:

issue_title: Incorrect image dimensions after reading in 3D+c TIFF
issue_body:
I'm trying to read the following file:

https://www.dropbox.com/s/0ynbscx4cmd5k91/E_z2_512_1um_CONTROL.tif?dl=1

skimage (ie tifffile) reads this correctly, but imageio doesn't — even though I thought it also used tifffile as a backend for tiffs?

```python
In [1]: import imageio
In [2]: from skimage import io
In [3]: im_iio = imageio.imread('E_z2_512_1um_CONTROL.tif')
In [4]: im_iio.shape
Out[4]: (159, 320, 512)

In [5]: im_ski = io.imread('E_z2_512_1um_CONTROL.tif')
In [6]: im_ski.shape
Out[6]: (53, 320, 512, 3)
```

What imageio is doing is reading the file in the array order present in the file (plane, channel, row, column), and squashing pln and ch dimensions together, while skimage is behaving as expected and producing two separate dimensions for pln and ch and (maybe a bit more than expected) moving ch to the last dimension, which is the dimension order expected by most scipy packages.

Any ideas about what is going wrong here?

issue_title: Inconsistent requirement of course id
issue_body:
`nbgrader fetch` and `nbgrader submit` both require that you specify the course id on the command line, and will throw an error if it is no. `nbgrader list`, however, does not require that the course id is specified on the command line, and if you forget to provide it, will just tell you there are no submissions/assignments without telling you that you forgot to give a course id. It would be nice if `nbgrader fetch` and `nbgrader submit` could optionally look for the course id in a config file (like `nbgrader list` does) and then not require students to type out the whole thing. I especially think this would be desirable on a system that only has one class -- in which case, it won't make sense to students why they have to type the name of the course as well as the name of the assignment.

Also, `nbgrader list` should probably check if the course id is empty, and if it is, give an error, rather than just saying nothing has been submitted/released.

issue_title: Notebooks marked unsaved on opening.
issue_body:
This is new behavior with the latest release 4.1.0 I open a notebook, and at the top of the title bar it states 

    Last Checkpoint: a few minutes ago (unsaved changes)

I press the save button, and confirm that the terminal prints out a save message. The header changes to:

    Last Checkpoint: a few seconds ago (autosaved)

I close the notebook window, then reopen the notebook. The top of the title bar states

    Last Checkpoint: a few seconds ago (unsaved changes)

In short, every time I open a notebook it is marked as unsaved.

I've been able to diagnose this. I have autosave turned off in my custom.js, where I have

    $([IPython.events]).on("notebook_loaded.Notebook", function () {
       IPython.notebook.set_autosave_interval(0);
    });

If I comment that out the notebook opens and immediately saves itself.

If this is the intended behavior I guess go ahead and just close this. But this is new behavior, it never happened before 4.1. I have autosave turned off because I run my notebooks in a dropbox directory, and in the past the notebooks and dropbox didn't always get along - you'd get errors on saves, and it is extremely annoying to deal with error messages every few minutes. 

I originally mentioned this on gitter. Carreau suggested I look for javascript errors. I see nothing  that stands out to me. I'll paste it in a reply if you are interested.

Environment: Windows 7 and 10, Notebook server 4.1.0, running on
Python 3.5.1 |Anaconda 2.4.1 (64-bit)| (default, Dec  7 2015, 15:00:12) [MSC v.1900 64 bit (AMD64)]

issue_title: Overriding ssl_options protocol and forcing TLSv1
issue_body:
Current code in [notebook/notebookapp.py#init_webapp](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/807f4501a2c507ee99076e9c724852519ca74412/notebook/notebookapp.py#L878) overrides user setting of ssl protocol version "ssl_version" and forces it to be TLSv1, so whatever the user configures in the jupyter_notebook_config file for this field will be ignored, and also some browsers like Chrome will say the connection uses an "obsolete cipher" when looking into the connection details.

A better approach for the whole ssl_context handling could be taking advantage of  an SSLContext object created through [ssl.create_default_context(*)](https://docs.python.org/3/library/ssl.html#ssl.create_default_context).
This function already provides safer defaults, which allows for protocol negotiation according to client/server capabilities while also declining SSLv2 and SSLv3. 

If it's not possible to use, due to SSLContext being on Python3.2+ and tornado that only allows a frozenset of keywords in the ssl_options dict, then it could be manually set to the user given value, as long as it is greater than [PROTOCOL_SSLV23](https://docs.python.org/3/library/ssl.html#ssl.PROTOCOL_SSLv23), which has a value of 2, so the more modern protocols will always have a greater value, like TLSv1_2 which is 5.

This last suggestion would be a simple and extensible fix. 
I'd be happy to submit a PR with such, after hearing your feedback on the issue.

issue_title: Integration with RELATE
issue_body:
I have written some online widgetry to support my classes. That system is called "RELATE", you can take a look here if interested:

https://github.com/inducer/relate

The basic "unit of teaching" there is called a "flow", and it presents a sequence of pages to the learner. What's on a page is pretty flexible--there are lots of options (multiple choice, python code, pdf submission, ...). The Python code submission experience could be better though, since every time a learner clicks "submit", a Docker container needs to be spun up, which leads to a 1-3 second delay, which is just enough to disrupt the nice "immediacy" that Python usually provides.

It would be really slick (at least IMO) if a Jupyter notebook could be offered up as one of the page types, along with some server-side logistics to spin up the containers running the kernels.

To my mind, Relate and Jupyter complement each other very nicely in a teaching setting. Relate's "run python" capability is basic, but it has a good story for authoring, data retention, access control to course content, and exam proctoring. Jupyter hits the opposite spot: It provides a great coding experience, but the classroom integration features are pretty basic.

So a first, basic question from my side would be: How friendly is the Jupyter codebase towards being embedded on the server and client sides (Relate is Django on the server side, but that can proxy for any wsgi app. Websockets I don't know so much about, but I assume that's likely solvable, too.) A simple client-side integration story would be to just let Jupyter manage a whole page (or an iframe) and hook into its "save" functionality. For what it's worth, the use case I have in mind is ~200-300 students in a classroom, all hacking away in their own notebooks. As in, I'd like the fan-out to individual little Docker containers on a zoo of backends to happen at the web worker level, but the scale is such that it'd likely be tricky to maintain a worker process per logged-in user.

I'd love to hear your thoughts on the feasibility of this. Also, if you're interested in devoting some resources to this, I'd love to chat--but that's not primarily what I'm asking about.

issue_title: Why not have <title></title> in the meta.html?
issue_body:
Every project I use meta_extended.html which looks like this:
```    
{% include "meta.html" %}
{% if meta.title %}<title>{{ meta.title }}</title>{% endif %}
```
I suggest add this to the django-meta: <title></title> tag is meta/seo tag too. For example, we can add property to the view mixin which enables/disables showing <title></title> in this way.
```
class MyView(MetadataMixin, View):
    title = 'Some page'
    title_tag = True # enables <title></title> in the meta.html
    description = 'This is an awesome page'
    image = 'img/some_page_thumb.gif'
    url = 'some/page/'
```
